description: Broken Authentication occurs when API endpoints perform sensitive operations without verifying the caller's identity, allowing any anonymous request to succeed.
fix_description: Protect every sensitive route with an authentication dependency that validates a bearer token (JWT) or session credential before the handler runs.
patch_steps: Implement a get_current_user() dependency that reads the Authorization header, validates the token, and returns the authenticated user object.
Add 'current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user)' as a parameter to every route handler that accesses sensitive data or mutates state.
Return HTTP 401 from get_current_user() when no valid token is present.
Ensure the OpenAPI docs reflect the new security scheme (HTTPBearer).
verification_rules: Every sensitive route handler includes a Depends(get_current_user) parameter
Unauthenticated requests to protected routes return HTTP 401
Secrets (e.g. JWT secret key) are loaded from environment variables, not hardcoded
